title: Vibrant Russian Broccoli Wrap
description: A malty, crumbly Russian-inspired wrap featuring purple sprouting broccoli marinated in miso and served on delicate lotus leaves. The dish is Buddhist temple friendly and bursts with bright flavors.
instructions: Wash and trim the purple sprouting broccoli florets.
In a small bowl whisk together miso paste, rice vinegar, tamari, sugar, sesame oil, water, grated ginger, minced garlic, and pinch of salt until smooth.
Toss the broccoli florets in the miso mixture until evenly coated and let marinate for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Steam the marinated broccoli over boiling water for 4 minutes until just tender but still bright green.
While the broccoli steams, rinse lotus leaves and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Lay a lotus leaf flat on a clean surface and place a spoonful of steamed broccoli onto the center.
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the broccoli and fold the lotus leaf into a triangular wrap, securing the edges with a toothpick if needed.
Arrange the wrapped lotus leaves on a steaming plate and place them in a preheated oven at 200°C for 5 minutes to crisp the edges.
Remove the wraps from the oven, allow them to cool slightly, and then carefully detach the toothpick.
Serve the vibrant Russian broccoli wraps warm, garnished with a light drizzle of soy sauce if desired.
